title: Vomiting / diarrhea
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

red_flags.text: Defines pediatric gastroenteritis use in DHA-licensed telehealth and excludes emergency and controlled-medication scenarios.
red_flags.rationale: Defines the exact pediatric Dubai telehealth scope and emergency exclusions.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Defines pediatric gastroenteritis use in DHA-licensed telehealth and excludes emergency and controlled-medication scenarios.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Gastroenteritis in Children – 07

red_flags.text: Prioritises red flags, dehydration, severe alternate illness, age, fever, bleeding, persistent vomiting, neurological abnormality, and instability.
red_flags.rationale: Directly supports mapped age, fever, bleeding, persistent vomiting, dehydration, neurological, and instability concern prompts.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Prioritises red flags, dehydration, severe alternate illness, age, fever, bleeding, persistent vomiting, neurological abnormality, and instability.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Gastroenteritis in Children – 07

red_flags.text: Lists age, fever, bleeding, persistent vomiting, dehydration, neurological, instability, caregiver, and worsening criteria for face-to-face assessment.
red_flags.rationale: Directly supports mapped escalation concerns and worsening review without autonomous referral output.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Lists age, fever, bleeding, persistent vomiting, dehydration, neurological, instability, caregiver, and worsening criteria for face-to-face assessment.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Gastroenteritis in Children – 07

investigations.text: Defines the acute gastroenteritis symptom pattern and limits laboratory studies to atypical or targeted presentations.

investigations.evidence_extract: Defines the acute gastroenteritis symptom pattern and limits laboratory studies to atypical or targeted presentations.

assessment.evidence_extract: Lists activity, consciousness, thirst, urine output, mucous membranes, pulse, capillary refill, skin turgor, heart rate, and respiratory signs.
